== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

revision.text:
'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇}} – the 11th Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."

=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

revision.text:
'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇}} – the 11th Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."

It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."

'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇}} – the 11th Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.

However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.

His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."

'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇}} – the 11th Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.

However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.

The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.

During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.

; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇}} – the 11th Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."

'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇}} – the 11th Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

revision.text:
'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇}} – the 11th Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

revision.text:
'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇}} – the 11th Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."

== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇}} – the 11th Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first aqqears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Uqqer Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇}} – the 11th Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first aqqears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Uqqer Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Uqqer Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."

'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇}} – the 11th Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first aqqears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Uqqer Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."

'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇}} – the 11th Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first aqqears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Uqqer Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."

'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇}} – the 11th Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first a11ears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: U11er Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."

== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

revision.text:
'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇}} – the 11th Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first a11ears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in 1175 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: U11er Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇}} – the ppth Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇}} – the ppth Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇}} – the ppth Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]
