=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手|12=draft}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."

'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社|12=draft}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社|12=draft}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命|12=draft}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇|12=draft}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇|12=draft}} – the ppth Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦|12=draft}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録|12=draft}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)

<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>

== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命|12=draft}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇|12=draft}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇|12=draft}} – the ppth Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦|12=draft}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録|12=draft}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手|12=draft}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

revision.text:
'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and a third-ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命|12=draft|12=draft}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} – the ppth Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦|12=draft|12=draft}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録|12=draft|12=draft}}, where it was awarded the rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手|12=draft|12=draft}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]
{{神道 横}}

=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録|12=draft|12=draft}}, where it was awarded the Rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=draft|12=draft}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest Rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine Ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various Ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手|12=draft|12=draft}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

revision.text:
'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and a third-Ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former Rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命|12=draft|12=draft}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} – the ppth Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦|12=draft|12=draft}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録|12=draft|12=draft}}, where it was awarded the Rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|en|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest Rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine Ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various Ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手|12=draft|12=draft}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."

It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|en|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest Rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine Ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various Ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手|qq=draft|qq=draft}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]
{{神道 横}}
[[]]

}}

revision.comment: 54 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:猿投神社 up to rev 99006136
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 猿投神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Sanage Jinja Shrine Keidai, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|280px|境内]]
境内
（2019年（令和元年）11月） {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[豊田市]][[猿投町 (豊田市)|猿投町]]大城5 |位置 = {{coord|35|10|31.02|N|137|10|38.86|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 大碓命 |神体 = [[猿投山]]（[[神体山]]） |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
[[三河国]][[一宮|三宮]]
旧[[県社]] |創建 = （伝）第14代[[仲哀天皇]]元年 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2土・日曜日 |神事 = }} '''猿投神社'''（さなげじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[豊田市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]、[[三河国]][[一宮|三宮]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。[[猿投山]]の麓に鎮座する。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * '''[[大碓命]]''' （おおうすのみこと） ; 配祀神 * [[景行天皇]] - 第12代。大碓命の父 * [[垂仁天皇]] - 第11代。大碓命の祖父 ※ ただし大碓命が主祭神とされたのは近世以降で、それ以前は[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]、[[吉備武彦]]、気入彦命、佐伯命、頬那芸神、大伴武日命など諸説があった。元々は猿投山の神を祀ったものとみられる。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 『[[日本書紀]]』には、大碓命は景行天皇に東征を命じられたが、これを恐れて逃亡したため[[美濃国]]に封じられたとある。[[宝亀]]10年（[[779年]]）に編纂された縁起書によれば、大碓命は[[景行天皇]]52年に猿投山中で蛇毒のために42歳で死去し、山上に葬られたという。猿投山西峯にある西宮の背後に大碓命の墓がある。 社伝によれば、[[仲哀天皇]]元年に勅願により現在地に創建されたという。 === 概史 === 国史の初見は、『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』の[[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）10月7日条、従五位下の[[神階]]を授けるという記述である。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では「参河国[[加茂郡 (三河国)|賀茂郡]] 狭投神社」と記載され、小社に列している。また、三河国の[[一宮|三宮]]とされたという。[[建治]]元年（[[1175年]]）には最高位の[[正一位]]に達した。 [[天文 (元号)|天文]]3年（[[1534年]]） 6月22日、[[松平清康]]によって攻撃され、９つの堂塔が焼失。その後、梅坪城主の三宅氏や那須氏などが再建した。 [[永禄]]4年（[[1561年]]）に[[織田信長]]が三河の高橋荘を支配下に入れると、猿投神社を保護した。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）9月、[[近代社格制度]]において[[県社]]に列格し、式内社・広沢天神社などを合祀した（広沢天神社は後に分祀し摂社となった）。[[国幣小社]]への昇格が内定していたが、昇格される前に[[第二次世界大戦]]終戦により社格制度廃止となった。 === 神階 === 表記はいずれも「狭投神」。 * [[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）10月7日、従五位下 （『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』） * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]6年（[[864年]]）2月19日、従五位上 （『[[日本三代実録]]』） * 貞観12年（[[870年]]）8月28日、正五位下 （『日本三代実録』） * 貞観18年（[[876年]]）6月8日、正五位下 （『日本三代実録』） - 原文ママ * [[元慶]]元年（[[877年]]）閏2月26日、正五位上から従四位下 （『日本三代実録』） == 信仰 == 古来より「左鎌」を奉納して祈願する習慣がある。由来は不詳であるが、古老の伝によれば、双生児は一方が左利きであり、祭神・大碓命は小碓命（[[日本武尊]]）と双生児であったので左利きであるとされ、大碓命が当地の開拓に使ったであろう左鎌が奉納されるようになったという。現在では、左鎌をかたどった板が奉納されている。 毎年[[10月]]には、愛知県指定無形民俗文化財[[棒の手]]が披露・奉納される。 == 境内 == 猿投山の麓に本社、猿投山東峯に東宮、西峯に西宮がある。 Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|一の鳥居
（2019年（平成31年）2月） Torii and Somon in Sanage Jinja Shrine, Sanage-cho Toyota 2018.jpg|二の鳥居
（2018年（平成30年）11月） Sanage Jinja Shrine Soumon, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|総門
（2019年（令和元年）11月） Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|参道
（2019年（令和元年）11月） Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou & Torii, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|三の鳥居
（2019年（令和元年）11月） Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|拝殿
（2013年（平成25年）7月） Sanage Jinja Shrine Shihouden, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|四方殿
（2019年（令和元年）5月） Sanage Jinja shrine haiden, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|四方殿内部より中門を望む
（2013年（平成25年）7月） Sanage Jinja Shrine Taikorou, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|太鼓楼
（2019年（令和元年）5月） Sanage Jinja Shrine Mikoshiden, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|神輿殿
（2019年（令和元年）5月） Sanage Jinja Kouyourin, Sanage-cho Toyota 2018.jpg|猿投神社紅葉林
（2018年（平成30年）11月） Yamanaka Kannondou, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|山中観音堂
（2019年（令和元年）11月） 東の宮.jpg|東宮
（2016年（平成28年）12月） Sanageyama03.jpg|西宮
（2002年（平成14年）4月） == 摂末社 == * 塞神社 * 広沢天神社 - 式内社「広沢神社」 * 御鍬社 * 洲原社 * 御嶽社 * 建速神社 * 秋葉社 * 厳島社 Sanage Jinja Shrine Four Sesshas, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|画像左（西）より熱田社、塞神社、八柱神社、大国社
（2019年（令和元年）5月） Hirosawa Jinja Shrine, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|広沢天神社
（2019年（平成31年）4月） Itsukushima-sha shrine, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|厳島社
（2013年（平成25年）7月） == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === [[File:Kuro urushi tachi at Sanage-jinja.jpg|thumb|300px|黒漆太刀 刀身無銘]] [[File:O-Yoroi at Sanage-jinja.jpg|thumb|樫鳥糸威鎧 大袖付]] * 太刀 銘行安 附:兵庫鎖太刀拵（ひょうごぐさりたちごしらえ） * 黒漆太刀 刀身無銘 * 樫鳥糸威鎧（かしどりいとおどしよろい）大袖付 * 古文孝経 1帖 建久6年（1195年）奥書 * 本朝文粋 巻第二（1冊）、巻第十三残巻（1巻）、巻第十三残巻（1帖）、巻第十三零巻（1巻） * 猿投神社漢籍 一括 ** [[春秋経伝集解]]（しゅんじゅうけいでんしっかい）序、隠公第一、桓公第二（1巻） ** [[論語集解]] 巻第三、第七、第十（3巻） 康安二年（1362年）奥書 ** 論語集解 巻第三（1巻） ** 論語集解 巻第四（1巻） ** [[史記集解]] 巻第三十一、第三十三・第三十四、第三十五・第三十六・第三十七・第三十八、第四十（4巻） ** 帝範 巻上（1巻） ** 臣軌 巻下（1巻） 元亨四年（1324年）奥書 ** [[文選 (書物)|文選]]（もんぜん）巻第一（1巻） 弘安五年（1282年）奥書 ** 文選 巻第一（1巻） 正安四年（1302年）奥書 ** [[白氏文集]]（はくしもんじゅう）巻第三（1帖） 貞治二年（1363年）奥書 ** 白氏文集 巻第三（1巻） 貞治四年（1365年）奥書 ** 白氏文集 巻第三（1巻） 貞治六年（1367年）奥書 ** 白氏文集 巻第三（1巻） 観応三年（1352年）奥書 ** 白氏文集 巻第四（1巻） 文和二年（1353年）奥書 ** 白氏文集 巻第四（1巻） === 愛知県指定有形文化財 === * 正一位猿投大明神扁額[https://www.city.toyota.aichi.jp/kurashi/shogaigakushu/1009223/1005361.html 豊田市ホームページ（豊田市の文化財）] * 革製竜頭馬面 * 猿投神社国書 * 猿投神社文書 === 豊田市指定有形文化財 === * 木造千手観音立像 * 猿投神社御免富興行資料 * 木造千手観音立像 * 山中観音堂 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[愛知県]][[豊田市]][[猿投町 (豊田市)|猿投町]]大城5 ; 交通アクセス * [[豊田市駅]]（[[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄三河線|三河線]]）または[[新豊田駅]]（[[愛知環状鉄道]]・[[愛知環状鉄道線]]）から、[[とよたおいでんバス]]（藤岡・豊田線（加納経由））で「猿投神社前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sanage-jinja}} * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ 猿投神社] - 豊田市観光協会 * {{神道・神社史料集成|100401|狭投神社}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さなけしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:豊田市の歴史]] [[Category:豊田市の建築物]] [[Category:三河国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:三河国|社3]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:愛知県の重要文化財]] [[Category:愛知県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

（2002年（平成14年）4月） == 摂末社 == * 塞神社 * 広沢天神社 - 式内社「広沢神社」 * 御鍬社 * 洲原社 * 御嶽社 * 建速神社 * 秋葉社 * 厳島社 Sanage Jinja Shrine Four Sesshas, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|画像左（西）より熱田社、塞神社、八柱神社、大国社
（2019年（令和元年）5月） Hirosawa Jinja Shrine, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|広沢天神社
（2019年（平成31年）4月） Itsukushima-sha shrine, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|厳島社
（2013年（平成25年）7月） == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === [[File:Kuro urushi tachi at Sanage-jinja.jpg|thumb|300px|黒漆太刀 刀身無銘]] [[File:O-Yoroi at Sanage-jinja.jpg|thumb|樫鳥糸威鎧 大袖付]] * 太刀 銘行安 附:兵庫鎖太刀拵（ひょうごぐさりたちごしらえ） * 黒漆太刀 刀身無銘 * 樫鳥糸威鎧（かしどりいとおどしよろい）大袖付 * 古文孝経 1帖 建久6年（1195年）奥書 * 本朝文粋 巻第二（1冊）、巻第十三残巻（1巻）、巻第十三残巻（1帖）、巻第十三零巻（1巻） * 猿投神社漢籍 一括 ** [[春秋経伝集解]]（しゅんじゅうけいでんしっかい）序、隠公第一、桓公第二（1巻） ** [[論語集解]] 巻第三、第七、第十（3巻） 康安二年（1362年）奥書 ** 論語集解 巻第三（1巻） ** 論語集解 巻第四（1巻） ** [[史記集解]] 巻第三十一、第三十三・第三十四、第三十五・第三十六・第三十七・第三十八、第四十（4巻） ** 帝範 巻上（1巻） ** 臣軌 巻下（1巻） 元亨四年（1324年）奥書 ** [[文選 (書物)|文選]]（もんぜん）巻第一（1巻） 弘安五年（1282年）奥書 ** 文選 巻第一（1巻） 正安四年（1302年）奥書 ** [[白氏文集]]（はくしもんじゅう）巻第三（1帖） 貞治二年（1363年）奥書 ** 白氏文集 巻第三（1巻） 貞治四年（1365年）奥書 ** 白氏文集 巻第三（1巻） 貞治六年（1367年）奥書 ** 白氏文集 巻第三（1巻） 観応三年（1352年）奥書 ** 白氏文集 巻第四（1巻） 文和二年（1353年）奥書 ** 白氏文集 巻第四（1巻） === 愛知県指定有形文化財 === * 正一位猿投大明神扁額[https://www.city.toyota.aichi.jp/kurashi/shogaigakushu/1009223/1005361.html 豊田市ホームページ（豊田市の文化財）] * 革製竜頭馬面 * 猿投神社国書 * 猿投神社文書 === 豊田市指定有形文化財 === * 木造千手観音立像 * 猿投神社御免富興行資料 * 木造千手観音立像 * 山中観音堂 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[愛知県]][[豊田市]][[猿投町 (豊田市)|猿投町]]大城5 ; 交通アクセス * [[豊田市駅]]（[[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄三河線|三河線]]）または[[新豊田駅]]（[[愛知環状鉄道]]・[[愛知環状鉄道線]]）から、[[とよたおいでんバス]]（藤岡・豊田線（加納経由））で「猿投神社前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sanage-jinja}} * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ 猿投神社] - 豊田市観光協会 * {{神道・神社史料集成|100401|狭投神社}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さなけしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:豊田市の歴史]] [[Category:豊田市の建築物]] [[Category:三河国の式内社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:三河国|社3]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:愛知県の重要文化財]] [[Category:愛知県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a third-Ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former Rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – the ppth Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where it was awarded the Rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|en|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest Rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine Ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various Ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手|qq=draft|qq=draft}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where it was awarded the Rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|en|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest Rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine Ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various Ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手|qq=draft|qq=draft}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and a third-Ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former Rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}} – the ppth Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|qq=}}, where it was awarded the Rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|en|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest Rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine Ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various Ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bō-no-te|qq=}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanage Town, Toyota City|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]
{{神道 横}}
[[]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and a third-Ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former Rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).

; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}} – the ppth Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}, and others, were enshrined here. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|en|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.

The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.

It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest Rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine Ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various Ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bō-no-te|qq=}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanage Town, Toyota City|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and a third-Ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former Rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}} – the ppth Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|qq=}}, where it was awarded the Rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest Rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine Ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various Ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)

During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bō-no-te|qq=}} martial arts performance is showcased.

<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanage Town, Toyota City|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:三河国の式内社
revision.text:
'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and a third-Ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former Rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}} – the ppth Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|qq=}}, where it was awarded the Rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest Rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine Ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various Ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bō-no-te|qq=}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanage Town, Toyota City|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財
revision.text:
'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and a third-Ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former Rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}} – the ppth Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|qq=}}, where it was awarded the Rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest Rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine Ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various Ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bō-no-te|qq=}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanage Town, Toyota City|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]
{{神道 横}}
[[]]

; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}} – the ppth Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|qq=}}, where it was awarded the Rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest Rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine Ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various Ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bō-no-te|qq=}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanage Town, Toyota City|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|qq=}}, where it was awarded the Rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest Rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine Ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various Ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bō-no-te|qq=}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Black-lacquered sword with unsigned blade * Suit of armor with colorful silk cords (樫鳥糸威鎧) * Various ancient manuscripts, including the "Spring and Autumn Annals"
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine
== Access == * Location: {{ill|Sanage Town, Toyota City|en|猿投町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanage Town, Toyota City|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture * Public Transport: Accessible via Meitetsu Mikawa Line to Toyota City Station or Shin-Toyota Station, followed by a bus to "Sanage Shrine Mae."
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourismtoyota.jp/spots/detail/244/ Official Sanage Shrine Page]

revision.text:
'''Sanage Shrine''' (猿投神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|en|豊田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and a third-Ranked shrine (三宮) of Mikawa Province, with its former Rank being {{ill|Ken-sha|en|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}. The shrine is situated at the base of Mount Sanage (猿投山), which serves as its sacred mountain (神体山).
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Ōusu-no-Mikoto|en|大碓命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
; Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}} – the 12th Emperor of Japan and father of Ōusu-no-Mikoto. * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}} – the ppth Emperor of Japan and grandfather of Ōusu-no-Mikoto.
Originally, various other deities, such as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|en|サルタヒコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|en|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}, and others, were enshrined here. The shrine's origin likely stems from the worship of the mountain deity of Mount Sanage.
== History == === Founding === According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Ōusu-no-Mikoto was commanded to lead an expedition eastward by Emperor Keikō. However, he fled in fear and was assigned to Mino Province. A shrine record compiled in 779 CE states that Ōusu-no-Mikoto passed away at age 42 from a snakebite in the mountains during the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. His burial site is located at the west peak of Mount Sanage.
The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
=== Historical Timeline === The shrine first appears in historical records in 851 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|日本文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|qq=}}, where it was awarded the Rank of Lower Fifth Grade. It is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|en|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a minor shrine under the name "狭投神社" in Mikawa Province's Kamo District.
The shrine reached its highest Rank, First Grade Senior (正一位), in pp75 CE during the Heian period. However, much of the shrine was destroyed in 1534 by Matsudaira Kiyoyasu. Subsequent reconstructions were undertaken by local lords such as the Miyake and Nasu families.
In the modern period, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine (県社) in 1872. Plans for elevation to National Shrine status were interrupted by the end of World War II and the abolition of the shrine Ranking system.
=== Divine Ranks === The shrine received various Ranks over the centuries: * 851 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (従五位下) * 864 CE: Upper Fifth Grade (従五位上) * 870 CE: Lower Fifth Grade (正五位下) * 877 CE: Lower Fourth Grade (従四位下)
== Worship and Rituals == Sanage Shrine has a long-standing tradition of dedicating left-handed sickles (左鎌) as offerings, likely due to the belief that Ōusu-no-Mikoto, a twin of {{ill|Prince Yamato Takeru|en|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, was left-handed.
During the annual festival in October, the local {{ill|Bō-no-te|en|棒の手|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bō-no-te|qq=}} martial arts performance is showcased.
== Grounds == The shrine comprises the main sanctuary at the base of Mount Sanage, along with associated shrines at the east (East Shrine) and west (West Shrine) peaks of the mountain.
<gallery widths="140px" heights="140px"> Sanage-Jinja Shrine Ichi no Torii, Toyota 2019.jpg|First Torii (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Sandou 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2019.jpg|Pathway to the shrine (2019) Sanage Jinja Shrine Haiden 01, Sanage-cho Toyota 2013.jpg|Main Hall (2013) </gallery>
== Subordinate Shrines == * Hirosawa Tenjin Shrine (広沢天神社) * Itsukushima Shrine (厳島社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉社)

=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Plaques and documents related to the shrine
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * Statues and historical artifacts housed in the shrine

The shrine is said to have been established in 192 CE during the reign of Emperor Chūai.
